zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John de Verdun, Baron of Westmeath於大約1226年出生在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾阿頓時，他的父親Theobald Le Boteler 2nd Chief Butler of Ireland27歲，母親Rohese de Verdun24歲。他於1244年8月14日之前與Eleanor de Bohun在英國英格蘭賀福爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1274年10月17日之前在愛爾蘭庫迪 維其可洛福阿可洛福，埋葬在英國斯塔福爾阿頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir John de Verdun, Baron of Westmeath于大约1226年出生在英国英格兰斯塔福爾阿頓时，他的父亲Theobald Le Boteler 2nd Chief Butler of Ireland27岁，母亲Rohese de Verdun24岁。他于1244年8月14日之前与Eleanor de Bohun在英国英格兰賀福爾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1274年10月17日之前在爱尔兰庫迪 維其可洛福阿可洛福，埋葬在英国斯塔福爾阿頓。

nameMeanings.name: Verdun,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French: habitational name from any of several places in France called Verdun. The placename is probably of Gaulish origin, deriving from ver(n) ‘alder’ + dūn ‘hill, fortress’.
Spanish and Catalan: habitational name from Verdú in Catalonia (Spain), which has the same origin as the French name (see 1 above).
English (of Norman origin): variant of   Verdon  , a cognate of 1 above. This surname is very rare in Britain.

variants.name: Virden
meanings: English: probably a variant of   Verdun  .

variants.name: Verdon
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Lucien, Marcel, Pierre, Renaud, Sylvie.
French: habitational name from (Le) Verdon, the name of several places in various parts of France, for example in Dordogne, Gironde, and Marne. Compare   Ferdon  .
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Verdun, the name of a fief in Vessey (Manche), France. Compare   Verdun  .

variants.name: Varden
meanings: English: variant of   Verdun  .

nameMeanings.name: Baron
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French, English, Dutch, Polish, Czech, Romanian, and Croatian; Breton (also Le Baron): from Old French barun, baron, Middle English barun, baroun, Middle Dutch baroen, Polish, Czech, Romanian, and dialectal Croatian baron ‘baron’ (i.e. a nobleman ranking below an earl and above a knight), a title of nobility which was in England also awarded to certain freemen of the cities of London and York and of the Cinque Ports. As a surname it is unlikely to be a status name denoting a person of rank. Generally, it referred to service in a baronial household or was acquired as a nickname by a peasant who had ideas above his station or applied to a man showing characteristics of a nobleman. See also 3 and 4 below, compare Barron and Lebaron .
French: habitational name from (Le) Baron, the name of several places in various parts of France. Compare   Lebaron  .
German: status name for a freeman or baron, from barūn ‘imperial or church official’, a loanword in Middle High German from Old French (see 1 above).

variants.name: Barron
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Jesus, Manuel, Francisco, Carlos, Miguel, Raul, Pedro, Ruben, Alfredo, Luis.

Scottish: status name from Scots baron, which in the Highlands denoted a small proprietary landholder. In Angus it alluded specifically to a tenant of one of the small baronies attached to the Abbey of Coupar Angus. The surname was taken by Scottish migrants to Ulster. Compare Baron .
Scottish: shortened form of McBarron, from Scottish Gaelic Mac a' Bharain ‘son of the baron’. See Baron and 1 above.

variants.name: Boron
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Krystyna, Edyta, Leszek, Mieczyslaw, Mieczyslawa, Wladyslaw.
Polish (Boroń): from a pet form of the personal names Borzysław or Borzymir, formed with the element bor ‘to fight’ (from Old Slavic boriti se).
French: habitational name from a place so called in Franche-Comté.

variants.name: Baro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Mario, Manuel, Ana, Angel, Carlos, Cesar, Fernando, Jesus, Jose, Orlando, Ramon, Rosalia.  Italian Guido, Angelo, Carmelo, Federico, Lucio.
Catalan (Baró): status name from baró ‘free man, baron’ (see Baron 1), or a habitational name from a place so named.
Hungarian (Báró): status name from báró ‘baron’. In the form Baró, it could be from a pet form of the personal names Barabás (see Barabas ) or Bertalan.

variants.name: Barona
meanings: Spanish: from the feminine form of Barón ‘baron’ (see Baron ), applied as a nickname or as an occupational name for a member of the household of a baroness.
Italian: probably from the feminine form of   Barone  . The name is concentrated in Sondrio province (Lombardy).

variants.name: Barren
meanings: English: probably a variant of   Baron   or   Barran  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.
Korean: variant of   O  .
Japanese: variant of Ō (see O ).

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: Sir
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: equivalent of George .

variants.name: George
meanings: Via Old French and Latin, from Greek Georgios (a derivative of geōrgos ‘farmer’, from gē ‘earth’ + ergein ‘to work’). This was the name of several early saints, including the shadowy figure who is now the patron of England (as well as of Germany and Portugal). If the saint existed at all, he was perhaps martyred in Palestine in the persecutions of Christians instigated by the Emperor Diocletian at the beginning of the 4th century. The popular legend in which the hero slays a dragon is a medieval Italian invention. He was for a long time a more important saint in the Orthodox Church than in the West, and the name was not much used in England during the Middle Ages, even after St George came to be regarded as the patron of England in the 14th century. Its use increased from the 1400s, and by 1500 it was regularly among the most popular male names. This popularity was reinforced when George I came to the throne in 1714 , bringing this name with him from Germany. It has been one of the most popular English boys' names ever since.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John de Verdun, Baron of Westmeath was born about 1226, in Alton, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Theobald Le Boteler 2nd Chief Butler of Ireland, was 27 and his mother, Rohese de Verdun, was 24. He married Eleanor de Bohun before 14 August 1244, in Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. He died before 17 October 1274, in Arklow, County Wicklow, Ireland, and was buried in Alton, Staffordshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir John de Verdun, Baron of Westmeath est né environ 1226, à Alton, Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Theobald Le Boteler 2nd Chief Butler of Ireland, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Rohese de Verdun, avait 24 ans. Il a épousé Eleanor de Bohun avant 14 août 1244, à Herefordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé avant 17 octobre 1274, à Arklow, County Wicklow, Irlande, et a été enterré à Alton, Staffordshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir John de Verdun, Baron of Westmeath um 1226 in Alton, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Theobald Le Boteler 2nd Chief Butler of Ireland 27 und seine Mutter Rohese de Verdun 24. Er hat vor 14. August 1244 in Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom Eleanor de Bohun geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Er starb vor 17. Oktober 1274 in Arklow, County Wicklow, Irland und wurde in Alton, Staffordshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John de Verdun, Baron of Westmeath è nato il circa 1226 nel Alton, Staffordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Theobald Le Boteler 2nd Chief Butler of Ireland, aveva 27 anni e il suo madre, Rohese de Verdun, aveva 24 anni. Lui sposato Eleanor de Bohun prima del 14 agosto 1244, nel Herefordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì prima del 17 ottobre 1274, nel Arklow, County Wicklow, Irlanda, e fu sepolto a Alton, Staffordshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir John de Verdun, Baron of Westmeathが約1226年にイギリスイギリススタフォードシュアイアアルトンで生まれた時，父Theobald Le Boteler 2nd Chief Butler of Irelandは27歳，母Rohese de Verdunは24歳であった。1244年8月14日以前にイギリスイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアでEleanor de Bohunと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1274年10月17日以前にアイルランドコウントイ ウィックロウアルクロウで死去し，イギリススタフォードシュアイアアルトンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John de Verdun, Baron of Westmeath(이)가 대략 1226년 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르 알턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Theobald Le Boteler 2nd Chief Butler of Ireland(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Rohese de Verdun(은)는 24세였다. 그는 1244년 8월 14일 이전 영국 잉글랜드 허퍼드실르에서 Eleanor de Bohun(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1274년 10월 17일 이전 아일랜드 카운티 윅로 아클로에서 사망했으며 영국 스태퍼드실르 알턴에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John de Verdun, Baron of Westmeath nasceu aproximadamente 1226, em Alton, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Theobald Le Boteler 2nd Chief Butler of Ireland, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Rohese de Verdun, tinha 24 anos. Ele casou-se com Eleanor de Bohun antes 14 de agosto de 1244, em Herefordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ele faleceu antes 17 de outubro de 1274, em Arklow, County Wicklow, Irlanda, e foi sepultado em Alton, Staffordshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John de Verdun, Baron of Westmeath родился около 1226 г. в местности алтон, стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Theobald Le Boteler 2nd Chief Butler of Ireland было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Rohese de Verdun, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Eleanor de Bohun перед 14 августа 1244 г. в местности хирефордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Он умер перед 17 октябрь 1274 г. в местности арклоу, коунти викклоу, Ирландия и был похоронен в местности алтон, стаффордшир, Англия.

sm.Drayton Bassett, Staffordshire, England,: Drayton Bassett, Staffordshire, Egelani, United Kingdom

es.Drayton Bassett, Staffordshire, England,: Drayton Bassett, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir John de Verdun, Baron of Westmeath nació aproximadamente 1226, en Alton, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Theobald Le Boteler 2nd Chief Butler of Ireland, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Rohese de Verdun, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Eleanor de Bohun antes de 14 de agosto de 1244, en Herefordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. Murió antes de 17 de octubre de 1274, en Arklow, County Wicklow, Irlanda, y fue sepultado en Alton, Staffordshire, Inglaterra.
